rows.notes: Page title/section heading centered at top. Very clear handwriting and no monetary figures on this line.
The word 'Civitati Oxon:' is legible. A '4' is clearly visible in the leftmost money column aligned with this entry (pounds). The shillings/pence to the right of it are faint/ambiguous so left blank.

Description reads 'Mayors Capons'. To the right there is a clear group '1 / 14 / 8' (pounds / shillings / pence) though the ink on shillings/pence is slightly blotchy; values fall in valid currency ranges.

Description uncertain (read as 'Curia Hustings'). There is a larger numeral near this line that appears to be '18' in the pounds column but the alignment is not crisp and shillings/pence are not legible. Low confidence because of ambiguity in the description and the digits.
Text reads 'Edi Christi' with '(Receiver)' written. A '4' in the pounds column lines up with this row but the smaller columns are faint; chosen to record only the clear pounds digit.
This is the following 'Do' (ditto) line referring to the previous item; no clear separate amounts are legible for this row (may be a sub-item). Description reads as 'Do (Subtreasurer)'.
Abbreviated Latin 'Coll. Omn. Anim.' is clear. The aligned amounts read '4 / 12 / 6' (pounds/shillings/pence) with clear column alignment and valid currency values.

Description 'Coll. Ball.' is legible. The pounds and shillings appear as '12 / 6' in the first two money columns, but the pence digit is either absent or not legible—only pounds and shillings recorded.
Line reads 'Coll. D. I. Bapt.' Similar, aligned numeric group reads '12 / 6' (pounds/shillings). Pence not legible; alignment and digits moderately clear.
Description is legible. The monetary columns clearly show '1 / 8 / 2' (pounds/shillings/pence). Alignment of the three vertical ruling lines is unambiguous here.
Text reads 'Supervision Ædificiorum 2+5'. The amounts to the right read '7 / 1 / 1'. Ink slightly heavier on the '1's but alignment and values are valid.
Entry reads 'Janitorii'. To the right the numbers read clearly '1 / 14 / 8' (pounds/shillings/pence); digits are crisp and within valid ranges.
Description 'Bibliothecario Dep. pro Anno' is readable. The amount clearly shows the repeating pattern '6 / 6 / 6' in the three money columns; alignment is very clear.
Reads 'Mitchelli Pens.' (pensions). The numeric trio '6 / 6 / 6' appears again and is well aligned. Slight ink blottiness on last digit but overall clear.
Description (abbrev.) reads 'Biblect. &c pro 5to Octobris'. The pounds/shillings appear as '6 / 4' though the pence column is faint; recorded those two clear values.
Line reads 'Concionatori, (Mr. Kingdom)'. A '1' is visible in the pounds column aligned with this row but the shillings/pence are not legible; moderate confidence.

rows.description: Vicar of Merton, a year to michl
rows.notes: Description 'Vicar of Merton, a year to michl' is legible. A clear '30' is visible in the pounds column for this line with no shillings/pence; digits are crisp and unambiguous.
Reads 'So. Hewington - do - do -'. The pounds figure '40' is clearly visible and centered in the pounds column; shillings/pence appear blank. Very clear alignment.

Word 'Pauperibus' clear. To the right there is a single clear '5' in the second narrow column (shillings column) aligned to this row; pounds and pence are not legible (likely 0 pounds and 5 shillings). Shillings value within valid range.
Description is clear. The amount column shows a '13' aligned with this line, located in the shillings column (no pounds digit visible). Might be 13 shillings; pounds/pence not legible.

rows.description: Land. Tax / North Half / to L. Day 1801
rows.notes: Reads 'Land. Tax / North Half / to L. Day 1801'. The aligned numbers read '3 / 12 / 8' across the three columns; digits are reasonably clear and valid for currency.
Line reads 'House & window tax - Do -'. The rightmost columns show '14 / 12 / 8' with decent legibility; alignment to the three ruling lines is consistent. Values are within valid ranges.
Description 'University Marshall' readable. The pounds and shillings appear as '1 / 3' but pence is faint or omitted; recorded the two clear values.

Final descriptive line is abbreviated and faint (read as '(orn Bries, the Year)'). The small numeric group to the right reads '1 / 9' across the narrow right columns — interpreted as 1 shilling 9 pence (no pounds visible). Moderate to low confidence due to abbreviated text and faint digits.
Final total at bottom right clearly shown as '126 / 12 / 9'. This Summa/total line is well aligned under the ruling lines and digits are clear.
